classifier_quote: Im Rahmen unseres SAP S/4HANA Transformationsprojekts
classifier_reason: Kärcher is an industrial manufacturer and the posting explicitly references an SAP S/4HANA transformation in its own SAP landscape.

evidence.quote: Im Rahmen unseres SAP S/4HANA Transformationsprojekts

brief.signal_summary: Kärcher is hiring a Senior SAP Platform Architect for its SAP Technology Team, and the posting explicitly places the role inside the company’s SAP S/4HANA transformation project with responsibility for complex SAP subprojects, architecture, and standards for hybrid SAP landscapes.
brief.why_now: This is a timely outreach moment because the evidence shows an active S/4HANA transformation, not a vague future plan. In S/4 programs, companies typically have to decide how much ECC history, legacy documents, and archive content must remain directly accessible versus what should be separated before migration. That decision often affects HANA sizing, migration scope, and the later effort to retire legacy environments. Where hybrid SAP landscapes are involved, the archive and legacy-data operating model also tends to become an architectural topic rather than just a storage topic.
brief.signal_quote: Im Rahmen unseres SAP S/4HANA Transformationsprojekts identifizierst du komplexe Problemstellungen und entwickelst nachhaltige, praktikable Lösungskonzepte, die sich eng an den SAP Best Practices orientieren.
stakeholder_hypothesis: Program Lead for SAP S/4HANA Transformation
Head of SAP Basis / SAP Operations
IT Director responsible for ERP landscape

angles.title: Hybrid SAP landscape standards create an archive operating-model decision
angles.detail: The role is tasked to 'definierst die Standards und Prozesse für den effizienten Betrieb unserer hybriden SAP-Landschaften'. That makes legacy data access and archive separation relevant as a landscape-design topic, not only a migration workstream.

angles.title: Platform-architecture ownership suggests legacy data may sit with the architecture team
angles.detail: Kärcher is hiring a '(Senior) SAP Platform Architect*in' inside the transformation. That role framing is a good entry to ask who owns decisions on ECC history, old documents, and post-migration access patterns.

angles.title: Complex SAP subprojects imply a need to avoid hidden legacy scope inside work packages
angles.detail: The posting says the role will 'steuerst Du als Projektleitung komplexe Teilprojekte im SAP-Umfeld und koordinierst die Implementierung von Arbeitspaketen'. In S/4 programs, legacy-data handling often cuts across multiple work packages and can stay implicit unless someone makes it explicit early.

discovery_questions: Within the SAP S/4HANA transformation project, has Kärcher already defined what portion of ECC historical data must remain available in daily operations?
For the hybrid SAP landscape, which team defines the target access model for legacy SAP data and archived documents?
Are legacy data and old-document decisions being handled centrally in the SAP Technology Team or inside individual SAP subprojects?
Does the architecture team expect to keep a legacy SAP environment running after migration for historical lookup, audit, or document access?
Which document-heavy processes matter most to preserve access to after cutover: for example service, finance, procurement, or manufacturing records?

email_slots.opener_de: im SAP S/4HANA-Transformationsprojekt: wie groß ist der Anteil des ECC-Bestands, den Ihr Team im Alltag tatsächlich noch braucht?

flags: The evidence is from a single role posting, so scope, timeline, and governance of the transformation are not confirmed beyond that posting.
The posting is architecture-focused; the final decision on legacy data and archiving could sit with a separate program office, enterprise architecture function, or central IT leadership.
No specific ECC system scope, migration phase, or business process scope is stated in the evidence.

rendered_email.subject: Datenaltlast vor dem S/4-Cutover bei Kärcher
rendered_email.body:
Hallo,

im SAP S/4HANA-Transformationsprojekt: wie groß ist der Anteil des ECC-Bestands, den Ihr Team im Alltag tatsächlich noch braucht?

Bei Maschinenbauern liegen erfahrungsgemäß 40 bis 60 Prozent der ECC-Datenbestände außerhalb der laufenden Nutzung. Wandern sie ungefiltert ins S/4-Sizing, kostet das im HANA-Footprint schnell einen sechsstelligen Betrag pro Jahr.

Wir haben für Kärcher eine kurze Potenzial-Einschätzung vorbereitet: welche Datenklassen sich vor der Migration archivieren oder stilllegen lassen und was das im Sizing typischerweise spart. Eine Seite, in zwei Minuten gelesen.

Soll ich sie Ihnen schicken? Ein kurzes Ja genügt.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
[SDR Name]

classifier_quote: Du wirkst aktiv an der Einführung von SAP S/4HANA mit
classifier_reason: Hamburger Hochbahn is an operating transport company, and the postings clearly show an internal SAP S/4HANA transformation project.

evidence.quote: Du wirkst aktiv an der Einführung von SAP S/4HANA mit

brief.signal_summary: Hamburger Hochbahn AG is hiring subproject leads for both SAP S/4HANA FI and SAP S/4HANA MM, indicating an active, cross-functional S/4HANA implementation with internal ownership and external implementation partners.
brief.why_now: When companies are in active S/4HANA implementation, decisions around what transactional history, accounting documents, and purchasing records remain in the new core versus move to an archive often get locked in implicitly by process design and sizing work. In public transport environments, FI and MM typically touch high-volume, long-retention document sets, so clarifying legacy-data scope early can reduce HANA footprint, avoid carrying unnecessary history into S/4, and create a cleaner path to decommissioning older ERP components later.
brief.signal_quote: Du wirkst aktiv an der Einführung von SAP S/4HANA mit und arbeitest in einem bereichsübergreifenden Team an diesem Großprojekt.
stakeholder_hypothesis: Program lead for SAP S/4HANA transformation
Procurement or Materials Management process owner

angles.title: FI subproject is shaping document scope now
angles.detail: The FI role explicitly covers SAP S/4HANA Finance areas including FI-GL, FI-AP, FI-AR and FI-AA. That makes finance document history, retention, and what should stay out of the new S/4 core a concrete discussion point during process design, not just after go-live.

angles.title: MM implementation raises purchasing-document legacy choices
angles.detail: The MM posting says the role leads the implementation in Materials Management and translates business requirements into S/4HANA processes. In that stream, legacy treatment for purchasing and materials-management documents is a practical design fork that can affect what is migrated versus archived.

angles.title: Cross-functional project creates a coordination issue between FI and MM
angles.detail: Both postings describe a central communication interface to business areas in a cross-functional S/4HANA program. That is a useful angle because archive and legacy-data rules often cut across FI and MM rather than fitting neatly into one workstream.

angles.title: External partners increase the need to pin down legacy boundaries early
angles.detail: The MM posting mentions coordination with external stakeholders such as consultants and implementation partners. In programs with multiple delivery parties, the boundary between active data in S/4 and legacy access/archive can otherwise remain ambiguous until late.

discovery_questions: In the S/4 program, who owns the decision on what FI and MM history must remain directly in S/4 versus accessible in an archive?
Has the team already defined a cutoff for legacy accounting and purchasing documents for the new S/4HANA core?
Are FI and MM using the same policy for historical document access, retention, and migration scope, or are those decisions still separate by workstream?
Is legacy-system decommissioning already part of the target picture after S/4 go-live, or is the current focus only on the implementation itself?
Which document classes are hardest to exclude from migration today: accounting documents, vendor invoices, purchasing documents, or asset-related records?

email_slots.opener_de: bei Ihrer SAP S/4HANA-Einführung laufen FI und MM parallel, während der Zuschnitt von Altdaten und Belegen den HANA-Umfang früh mitfestlegt.

classifier_quote: Im Zuge der Transformation von SAP IS-U zu SAP S/4 Utilities
classifier_reason: This is a utility end user and the posting explicitly mentions an SAP IS-U to S/4 Utilities transformation, which is a strong buying signal for archiving/data reduction needs.

evidence.quote: Im Zuge der Transformation von SAP IS-U zu SAP S/4 Utilities

brief.signal_summary: enercity Netz GmbH is hiring a temporary Head of Meter Data Management & SAP Reporting through 31.12.2027, and the posting explicitly ties the role to a transformation from SAP IS-U to SAP S/4 Utilities while maintaining stable meter-data processes and SAP-based reporting.
brief.why_now: The posting shows an active SAP IS-U to SAP S/4 Utilities program, with operational accountability for metering, billing/prognosis processes, and reporting running in parallel. In S/4 utility migrations, companies typically have to decide early which legacy operational data and documents stay active, which move to archive, and how historical data will be exposed to reporting without inflating the new HANA footprint. That matters even more when process stability and analytics modernization are both in scope at the same time.
brief.signal_quote: Im Zuge der Transformation von SAP IS-U zu SAP S/4 Utilities begleitest du die Weiterentwicklung datengetriebener Prozesse bis hin zur Stärkung moderner Reporting- und Analyticsansätze, insbesondere im Umfeld von Power BI, Datasphere und SAC.
stakeholder_hypothesis: Head of SAP / SAP Program Lead
Program Manager for SAP IS-U to SAP S/4 Utilities transformation
IT Lead for Billing and Forecasting Processes
Data / Analytics Lead for Power BI, Datasphere and SAC

angles.title: Meter data continuity during IS-U to S/4 Utilities
angles.detail: The role owns 'Messdatenprozesse für Abrechnung und Prognose' during the SAP IS-U to SAP S/4 Utilities transformation. That creates a concrete opening around how much historical metering-related data must remain directly accessible in the new core versus served from archive for audit, customer, or reconciliation needs.

angles.title: Reporting modernization could pull legacy data into the new stack
angles.detail: The posting explicitly connects the transformation to 'Power BI, Datasphere und SAC' plus SAP-based reporting. A relevant angle is whether older IS-U-era reporting data and documents are being replicated into the new analytics landscape by default, or selectively retained outside the transactional core.

angles.title: Transformation governance runs through 31.12.2027
angles.detail: The job is 'befristet bis 31.12.2027', which suggests the transformation and stabilization phase has a defined multi-year horizon. That makes timing conversations concrete: archive/legacy decisions made late can force avoidable data into the S/4 scope and complicate later legacy decommissioning.

discovery_questions: For meter data processes supporting billing and forecasting, which historical data sets must remain day-to-day accessible after the move to SAP S/4 Utilities?

classifier_quote: Auswahl und Einführung eines neuen ERP-Systems
classifier_reason: The company appears to be an end user and is explicitly planning a major ERP transformation, which is a strong buying signal.

evidence.quote: Auswahl und Einführung eines neuen ERP-Systems

brief.signal_summary: AES Aircraft Elektro/Elektronik System GmbH is hiring an ERP project lead for a strategically important transformation program, with responsibility for selecting, planning, and implementing a new ERP system over the coming years.
brief.why_now: A company that is selecting and introducing a new ERP system typically has to decide early which historical ERP data and legacy documents must stay operational in the target environment versus what can be archived separately. Those decisions can materially affect target architecture, migration scope, testing effort, and long-term legacy system costs if they are left implicit until late in planning.
brief.signal_quote: Für ein strategisch bedeutendes Zukunftsprojekt suchen wir einen ERP-Projektleiter (m/w/d), der die Auswahl und Einführung eines neuen ERP-Systems in den kommenden Jahren verantwortet.
stakeholder_hypothesis: Head of ERP Applications / ERP Program Manager
Head of Finance Process or Controlling

angles.title: ERP selection phase is still open
angles.detail: The posting says the new ERP system is still being selected and introduced. That is a concrete window to shape how legacy ERP data and historical documents are separated from operational scope before the future-state architecture hardens.

angles.title: Multi-year program creates legacy retention risk
angles.detail: AES describes the ERP initiative as running "in den kommenden Jahren". In longer ERP programs, the question of what must remain accessible day-to-day from the old world versus what can move into compliant archive access often expands unless ownership is set early.

angles.title: Aerospace electronics documentation footprint
angles.detail: As an aerospace electronics company, AES likely works with long-lived technical, quality, procurement, and production-related records. In an ERP replacement, clarifying which of those records need transactional access in the new ERP versus archive access can prevent unnecessary carryover into the new platform.

discovery_questions: Who owns the decision in the ERP program on what historical ERP data and old documents need operational access after go-live?
Is the team already defining the cut between data that must move into the new ERP and data that can remain accessible via archive access?
During ERP selection, is legacy data scope being treated as part of target architecture and sizing, or postponed until migration planning?
Which historical record types are most business-critical to keep available day-to-day: purchasing, production, quality, or finance documents?
Is the future goal to keep any legacy ERP environment running for historical lookups, or to reduce dependence on old systems after cutover?

brief.why_now: When companies evolve their SAP landscape while running broader manufacturing transformation programs, legacy data decisions often become time-sensitive. In S/4-era programs generally, teams have to decide which operational history must stay in the new core, what can be archived compliantly, and how old documents remain accessible if legacy applications are later retired. Those choices matter especially where planning, production control, and maintenance teams depend on historical records day to day.
brief.signal_quote: Du hast Lust die Weiterentwicklung unserer SAP-Landschaft zu verantworten und somit als wichtige Ansprechperson für die Fachbereiche Planung, Fertigungssteuerung und Instandhaltung zu agieren?

angles.title: Production planning and maintenance footprint in SAP
angles.detail: The SAP in-house role explicitly covers 'SAP PP, SAP ePP/DS und SAP PM' and names 'Planung, Fertigungssteuerung und Instandhaltung'. That makes legacy access for planners, production control, and maintenance a concrete conversation, not an abstract IT topic.

angles.title: Transformation is connected to operations leadership
angles.detail: The Global Manager Manufacturing Transformation role is described as 'Sparringspartner des COO, der Operations-Leitung und der Business Heads'. That suggests decisions around historical production and plant-related information may need operational sponsorship, not just IT ownership.

angles.title: Global transformation with site changes and production moves
angles.detail: The manufacturing transformation posting mentions 'globale Transformationsprogramme, Standortentwicklungen, Produktionsverlagerungen sowie Technologie- und Automatisierungsinitiativen'. angles.detail: The posting explicitly combines "ERP/ECM/DMS/ITSM" in one handlungsfeld. That makes a legacy-data discussion more concrete: who defines which historical SAP-linked documents remain directly accessible in ERP processes versus being served from archive/ECM after transformation.

angles.title: Process design ownership for ERP-SAP
angles.detail: The role "gestaltest und steuerst Unternehmensprozesse" and is Ansprechpartner for the ERP-SAP handlungsfeld. That is a useful anchor to discuss whether retention, archive access, and old-document retrieval are being designed as part of process design rather than left until cutover.

angles.title: Training and support responsibility for ERP-SAP users
angles.detail: Because the role owns "Schulungs- und Supportprozesse für ERP-SAP Anwender", a practical angle is user day-to-day need for old ERP content: which historic transactions and documents users still need directly, and which can shift to archive access without hurting supportability.

discovery_questions: In your ERP-SAP handlungsfeld, who owns the decision on historical SAP data and old business documents: SAP process owners, ECM/DMS, or a central architecture team?
For ERP-SAP users, which historical records are still needed day-to-day in support and training scenarios, and which are mainly retained for audit or lookup?
Because the role spans ERP and ECM/DMS, are archive access requirements already part of process design, or handled later as a separate workstream?
Is the target discussion mainly about keeping history accessible for users, reducing active ERP scope, or preparing eventual retirement of older platforms?

email_slots.opener_de: im ERP-SAP Handlungsfeld: wie groß ist der Anteil des ECC-Altbestands, den Ihr Team im Alltag tatsächlich noch braucht?

flags: The evidence does not explicitly name S/4HANA; the signal is ERP/SAP-focused rather than an explicitly stated S/4 program.
The same job posting appears multiple times, so the evidence base is effectively one role repeated.
The title could indicate a consulting or cross-functional SAP role; confirm whether the scope is internal enterprise ERP, customer-facing delivery, or both before tailoring outreach too narrowly.

rendered_email.subject: Datenaltlast vor dem S/4-Cutover bei Konica Minolta
rendered_email.body:
Hallo,

im ERP-SAP Handlungsfeld: wie groß ist der Anteil des ECC-Altbestands, den Ihr Team im Alltag tatsächlich noch braucht?

Bei IT-Dienstleistern liegen erfahrungsgemäß 40 bis 60 Prozent der ECC-Datenbestände außerhalb der laufenden Nutzung. Wandern sie ungefiltert ins S/4-Sizing, kostet das im HANA-Footprint schnell einen sechsstelligen Betrag pro Jahr.

Wir haben für Konica Minolta eine kurze Potenzial-Einschätzung vorbereitet: welche Datenklassen sich vor der Migration archivieren oder stilllegen lassen und was das im Sizing typischerweise spart. Eine Seite, in zwei Minuten gelesen.

Soll ich sie Ihnen schicken? Ein kurzes Ja genügt.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
[SDR Name]

classifier_quote: GIB Suite für SAP ECC, GIB SCX für S/4 HANA
classifier_reason: ifm is an industrial manufacturer and the posting shows it operates SAP ECC and S/4-related manufacturing systems, indicating an ERP landscape transformation context.

postings.title: Software Developer SAP ABAP (m/w/d) - Schwerpunkt Manufacturing

evidence.quote: GIB Suite für SAP ECC, GIB SCX für S/4 HANA

brief.signal_summary: ifm is hiring a SAP ABAP developer focused on manufacturing, explicitly working across "GIB Suite für SAP ECC" and "GIB SCX für S/4 HANA" while optimizing and simplifying SCM processes and maintaining the SAP development environment.
brief.why_now: This is a strong ERP transformation signal because the posting references both ECC- and S/4-based manufacturing tooling in the same role. In S/4 programs, companies typically have to decide early which legacy transactional and document-heavy data stays in the live HANA footprint, what moves into compliant archive access, and how much historical manufacturing context users still need day to day. Those choices often affect sizing, migration effort, and the path to later legacy system retirement.
brief.signal_quote: Weiterentwicklung unserer SAP Add-ons mit dem Schwerpunkt im Bereich Fertigung / Manufacturing (GIB Suite für SAP ECC, GIB SCX für S/4 HANA)
stakeholder_hypothesis: Head of SAP / SAP Competence Center

angles.title: ECC and S/4 manufacturing tools are named in one role
angles.detail: The posting explicitly references both "GIB Suite für SAP ECC" and "GIB SCX für S/4 HANA". That makes the handoff between active manufacturing data and legacy-access needs a concrete discussion point, not a generic SAP topic.

angles.title: Manufacturing process focus raises historical production-data access questions
angles.detail: This role centers on "Fertigung / Manufacturing" and requires understanding of "Prozessen der Fertigung sowie des SAP PP Standards". In that context, old production-related records and documents often remain relevant for planners, plants, or support teams even after system changes.

angles.title: SCM simplification can conflict with keeping old data in the operational core
angles.detail: The posting says the team supports optimization and simplification of SCM processes. A useful angle is whether simplification in the S/4 target includes separating historical ECC-era data and documents from the live operational environment rather than carrying them forward by default.

angles.title: Documentation and development environment ownership suggests governance concerns
angles.detail: The role includes "Sicherstellung einer aussagekräftigen Dokumentation" and maintenance/optimization of the SAP development environment. That creates a relevant opening around governed retention and auditable access to older SAP-linked documents in a transformed landscape.

discovery_questions: How far is ifm in deciding what manufacturing history must remain directly accessible in the S/4-oriented environment versus archive access?
Which team currently owns legacy data and document decisions for the ECC-to-S/4-related manufacturing landscape?
Are production and SCM users expected to work with historical ECC-era records day to day after the target landscape is simplified?
Is the current focus mainly on process/application build-out, or has data volume and historical document scope already been addressed?
How are compliance and documentation requirements being handled for older SAP-linked manufacturing records if systems are later consolidated or retired?

email_slots.opener_de: bei Ihrer Fertigungslandschaft mit GIB Suite für SAP ECC und GIB SCX für S/4 HANA wird der Schnitt bei Altbelegen oft schon durch die Zielarchitektur vorgeprägt.

flags: Only one posting is provided; it signals SAP manufacturing landscape work but does not explicitly confirm a named S/4 transformation program.
The hiring entity in the snippet is "ifm business solutions gmbh", so decision-making could sit in a group IT or subsidiary structure rather than one central team.
No timeline, migration phase, or archive/compliance initiative is explicitly stated in the evidence.

Wir haben für ifm eine kurze Potenzial-Einschätzung vorbereitet: welche Datenklassen sich vor der Migration archivieren oder stilllegen lassen und was das im Sizing typischerweise spart. Eine Seite, in zwei Minuten gelesen.
